description: Nine-run protected sequencing scan on the no-distance edge-graph control branch. The C0.6 positive-mismatch forcing schedules are kept fixed, but once mismatch accumulation starts the survivor update is forced into an alternating adjacency versus graph-shell regime and explicit composition-level inconsistency is counted.
architecture_notice: This branch keeps graph size, adjacency and graph-shell kernels, the clustered / corridor / triad representative families, the projected-transverse sector, and the shell-weight measurement vocabulary fixed. Only the post-mismatch update rule changes: survivor updates are deliberately alternated across adjacency and graph-shell kernels.
baseline_notice: C0.7 keeps the corrected C0.6 forcing schedules and the protected topology-stability rule active, but adds an explicit composition-level inconsistency probe. The key quantity is the disagreement between adjacency-to-shell and shell-to-adjacency survivor refinements on the same forced graph.

run.notes: Noise-seeded inconsistency forcing on the clustered composite anchor.

forcing_protocol.label: Noise-seeded inconsistency forcing
forcing_protocol.definition: Use the exact C0.3 incidence-noise ladder as the forcing schedule, then deliberately alternate adjacency and graph-shell survivor updates once mismatch is live.

forcing_protocol.label: Hybrid seeded inconsistency forcing
forcing_protocol.definition: At each step compare the scheduled noise and motif forcings, then run the alternating-kernel inconsistency probe on the protected candidate with the strongest failure signal.
